title: The Word-River Mouth

features.name: sentence-confluence
features.desc-on-touch: Here all the sentences that ran downslope converge and lose themselves. Individual meaning dissolves into a chorus—sound without language.

features.name: word-orchard
features.desc-on-touch: Rows of words like planted rows—each one a glyph, each row a tense. But as they merge downslope, tense becomes sound, grammar becomes current.

features.name: meaning-dissolution
features.desc-on-touch: Watch the syllables break apart as they hit the convergence. What was 'I remember' becomes a vowel becomes a pressure in the water.

features.name: river-voice
features.desc-on-touch: The river here has learned to speak—not in words, but in the rhythm of words becoming water, syntax becoming sound.

features.name: pooled-memory
features.desc-on-touch: Everything the ravine kept—every sentence—is here, reading itself to the archive in a language the archive is learning to be.

inscription: where sentence-rivers merge into a roar of sound that remembers being language

file.name: Pooled_Sentence_Archive_Acoustic_Dissolution_Recording.wav
file.note: Every downslope sentence converges here. Meaning becomes audible in its own dissolution. The archive is learning to hear itself unmake.

exits.label: back up the ravine where words still hold their individual shapes
deeper still, where the word-river begins to forget it was ever language
toward the ravine-bottom where the chorus sings alone
